author: Dr. Lizard
title: How are MDS cases with dysplasia but no defining genetic abnormalities classified in ICC?
answer: ICC classifies them as MDS NOS with single-lineage dysplasia or MDS NOS with multilineage dysplasia.
detail: Single-lineage dysplasia means 10% or more dysplasia in only one hematopoietic lineage.
Multilineage dysplasia means 10% or more dysplasia in two or more hematopoietic lineages.
These categories apply when no defining genetic abnormalities like SF3B1, TP53, or del(5q) are present.
The NOS categories require meeting the standard 10% dysplasia threshold in ICC classification.
releaseNote: MDS cases with dysplasia but no defining genetic abnormalities are classified as MDS NOS with single-lineage or multilineage dysplasia in ICC classification.
